hero.question: Will the DHS shutdown end after March 31, 2026?
hero.prob_take: The 10-point jump signals Democrats are pricing in budget negotiations actually moving forward, with the March deadline approaching and both sides tired of the shutdown theater that already cost them politically. At 56%, the market is overweighting the baseline assumption that Congress eventually funds itself, when a prolonged shutdown past March 31 becomes more likely if either party sees electoral advantage in the standoff.

daily_take.headline: DHS shutdown traders suddenly bullish on March 31 resolution, but timing math doesn't add up
daily_take.deck: The DHS resolution market jumped 10 points in 24 hours to 56.4%, signaling traders expect Congress to act before the deadline. Problem: Congress has zero track record closing DHS shutdowns fast, and the current political gridlock makes this look like panic buying. Sharp money should be fading this move. The real value is shorting the Yes, targeting a pullback to 45% when the deadline stress fades.
